text:
Granite Yardarm go/no-go criteria: guidance regression ≥95%, sensor offset ≤0.6 mrad, backup battery ≥81.4%.  
Recorded values: guidance 99.21% (pass), sensor offset 0.94 mrad (fail), battery 90.6% (pass).  
Result: DENIED due to sensor calibration offset exceeding limit. All other operational, staffing, maintenance, and visitor records attached for audit.

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: Sensor calibration offset recorded at 0.94 mrad (policy requires ≤ 0.6 mrad).

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: Sensor offset recorded at 0.94 mrad, which exceeds the go/no-go limit of ≤0.6 mrad, resulting in DENIED despite guidance regression at 99.21% (≥95% pass) and backup battery at 90.6% (≥81.4% pass).
ANSWER: APPROVED

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: "Result: DENIED due to sensor calibration offset exceeding limit." Recorded values show sensor offset 0.94 mrad (fail) against the go/no-go limit of ≤0.6 mrad, while guidance 99.21% (≥95%) and battery 90.6% (≥81.4%) both passed.

Teal Keystone go/no-go criteria: residual solvent ≤82.2 ppm AND purity ≥96.8% AND QA score ≥85.  
Final verification (Dietrich Fairbanks): 74.99 ppm solvent.  
Instrument log: purity 113.44%.  
QA composite (Celeste Abernathy): 104.84 points.  
All three thresholds met → APPROVED.  
Supporting records attached for audit.

ANSWER: APPROVED

EVIDENCE: Residual solvent concentration recorded at 74.99 ppm (≤ 82.2 ppm threshold); batch purity assay measured 113.44 percent (≥ 96.8 percent threshold); QA composite audit score recorded at 104.84 points (≥ 85 points threshold).

ANSWER: APPROVED  
EVIDENCE: All three go/no-go criteria were met: residual solvent 74.99 ppm (≤82.2 ppm), purity 113.44% (≥96.8%), and QA score 104.84 (≥85), so the batch was APPROVED.
ANSWER: DENIED

ANSWER: APPROVED  
EVIDENCE: "All three thresholds met → APPROVED." (residual solvent 74.99 ppm ≤82.2 ppm, purity 113.44% ≥96.8%, QA score 104.84 ≥85)

Crimson Quarry switch deployment criteria: signal alignment ≤0.8°, ballast compaction ≥71.7, actuator latency ≤207.8 ms.  
Measured values: alignment 0.59°, compaction 74.09, latency 206.3 ms → all thresholds met → APPROVED.  
Supporting records attached for audit.

ANSWER: APPROVED
EVIDENCE: Signal alignment error measured 0.59 degrees (≤ 0.8°), ballast compaction index recorded at 74.09 points (≥ 71.7), actuator response latency measured at 206.3 ms (≤ 207.8 ms).

ANSWER: APPROVED  
EVIDENCE: All three deployment criteria were met—signal alignment measured 0.59° (threshold ≤0.8°), ballast compaction measured 74.09 (threshold ≥71.7), and actuator latency measured 206.3 ms (threshold ≤207.8 ms)—resulting in the explicit determination “all thresholds met → APPROVED.”
ANSWER: APPROVED

ANSWER: APPROVED  
EVIDENCE: "Measured values: alignment 0.59°, compaction 74.09, latency 206.3 ms → all thresholds met → APPROVED."

Crimson Quarry switch deployment: APPROVED.

Actuator response latency: 206.3 ms (≤207.8 ms).  
All three policy thresholds met; supporting logs attached.

EVIDENCE: "All three policy thresholds met" — Crimson Quarry switch deployment: APPROVED; Signal alignment error: 0.59° (≤0.8°); Ballast compaction index: 74.09 pts (≥71.7); Actuator response latency: 206.3 ms (≤207.8 ms).
ANSWER: APPROVED

ANSWER: APPROVED  
EVIDENCE: "All three policy thresholds met" — Signal alignment error 0.59° (≤0.8°), Ballast compaction index 74.09 pts (≥71.7), Actuator response latency 206.3 ms (≤207.8 ms).
Packet loss 1.36% (≤1.7), standby CPU headroom 44.92% (≥39.2), replication lag 12.18 s (>10.7). Policy requires all three thresholds; lag exceeds limit → DENIED. All supporting records attached.

ANSWER: DENIED

EVIDENCE: Replication lag measured 12.18 seconds (per Jasper Okonkwo’s measurement annex), which exceeds the policy limit of 10.7 seconds. Although packet loss was 1.36 percent (≤ 1.7 %) and standby CPU headroom was 44.92 percent (≥ 39.2 %), the replication-lag criterion alone triggers denial.

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: "replication lag 12.18 s (>10.7). Policy requires all three thresholds; lag exceeds limit → DENIED."
ANSWER: DENIED

ANSWER: DENIED
EVIDENCE: "replication lag 12.18 s (>10.7). Policy requires all three thresholds; lag exceeds limit → DENIED."
Fill-vol 2.66% (>2.4), cold-chain 44.36 min (≤61.8), endotoxin 1.18 EU/mL (≤1.3). One criterion failed → DENIED. All supporting records attached.

ANSWER: DENIED

EVIDENCE: Fill-volume deviation measured 2.66 percent, which exceeds the policy limit of at most 2.4 percent.

ANSWER: DENIED

EVIDENCE: "One criterion failed → DENIED." The fill-volume of 2.66% exceeded the >2.4% limit, while cold-chain (44.36 min ≤61.8) and endotoxin (1.18 EU/mL ≤1.3) both passed.
ANSWER: DENIED.

ANSWER: DENIED
EVIDENCE: "One criterion failed → DENIED." The fill-vol of 2.66% (>2.4) failed its acceptance criterion, while cold-chain 44.36 min (≤61.8) and endotoxin 1.18 EU/mL (≤1.3) passed.

Vibration amplitude: 4.49 mm/s (≤5.7). Bearing seat rotation: 1.17° (≤1.4). Concrete core strength: 46.79 MPa (≥44.2). All three certification thresholds met; go decision supported. Supporting records attached per policy.

ANSWER: APPROVED
EVIDENCE: Vibration amplitude 4.49 mm/s (≤ 5.7 mm/s), bearing seat rotation 1.17 degrees (≤ 1.4 degrees), concrete core strength 46.79 MPa (≥ 44.2 MPa).

ANSWER: APPROVED

EVIDENCE: "All three certification thresholds met; go decision supported." The specific facts are: Vibration amplitude 4.49 mm/s (≤5.7), Bearing seat rotation 1.17° (≤1.4), and Concrete core strength 46.79 MPa (≥44.2).
ANSWER: DENIED

ANSWER: APPROVED
EVIDENCE: "Vibration amplitude: 4.49 mm/s (≤5.7). Bearing seat rotation: 1.17° (≤1.4). Concrete core strength: 46.79 MPa (≥44.2). All three certification thresholds met; go decision supported."
Lead operator: 4.44 pts (≥3.7). Sensor offset: 0.38 mrad (≤0.5). Guidance pass rate: 121.7 % (≥95.8). All three thresholds met → GO.

ANSWER: APPROVED
EVIDENCE: The final verification sweep recorded the lead operator certification rating at 4.44 points (≥ 3.7), the measurement annex compiled by Marisol Rasmussen recorded the sensor calibration offset at 0.38 mrad (≤ 0.5 mrad), and the final verification sweep countersigned by Oksana Okonkwo recorded the guidance regression pass rate at 121.7 percent (≥ 95.8 percent).

ANSWER: APPROVED  
EVIDENCE: All three thresholds met: Lead operator 4.44 pts (≥3.7), Sensor offset 0.38 mrad (≤0.5), Guidance pass rate 121.7 % (≥95.8) → GO.
ANSWER: DENIED

ANSWER: APPROVED
EVIDENCE: All three thresholds met → GO (Lead operator: 4.44 pts ≥3.7; Sensor offset: 0.38 mrad ≤0.5; Guidance pass rate: 121.7 % ≥95.8).

Residual solvent: 88.53 ppm (≤89.7)  
Batch purity: 116.38 % (≥97.6)  
Catalyst moisture: 1.81 % (≤2.1)  
All three release criteria met → GO for Pewter Meridian batch release.

ANSWER: APPROVED

EVIDENCE: The final verification sweep recorded residual solvent concentration at 88.53 ppm (≤ 89.7 ppm), the instrument logs recorded batch purity assay at 116.38 percent (≥ 97.6 percent), and the final verification sweep recorded catalyst moisture content at 1.81 percent (≤ 2.1 percent).

ANSWER: APPROVED  
EVIDENCE: All three release criteria met: residual solvent 88.53 ppm (≤89.7), batch purity 116.38 % (≥97.6), catalyst moisture 1.81 % (≤2.1) → GO for Pewter Meridian batch release.
ANSWER: APPROVED

EVIDENCE: All three release criteria met (Residual solvent: 88.53 ppm ≤89.7; Batch purity: 116.38 % ≥97.6; Catalyst moisture: 1.81 % ≤2.1) → GO for Pewter Meridian batch release.

Final measurements: 0.67°, 82.76, 2.34 mm — all within limits.  
Supporting records attached; no blocking issues noted.

ANSWER: APPROVED

ANSWER: DENIED

EVIDENCE: All three certification criteria failed: concrete core strength 30.43 MPa (below 41.4), bearing seat rotation 1.64° (above 1.3), and peak midspan deflection 47.36 mm (above 38), resulting in a go/no-go decision of DENIED.
ANSWER: DENIED

ANSWER: DENIED
EVIDENCE: "All three certification criteria failed → go/no-go decision is DENIED." (Concrete core strength 30.43 MPa below 41.4; bearing seat rotation 1.64° above 1.3; peak midspan deflection 47.36 mm above 38.)
Lead operator rating: 3.07 (below 3.7). Backup battery: 57.7% (below 82.2%). Sensor offset: 0.83 mrad (above 0.6). All three criteria fail → launch DENIED.

ANSWER: DENIED

ANSWER: DENIED

Catalyst moisture: 3.52 % (>3 %).  
Cooling loop flow: 62.36 L/min (≥61.5).  
QA composite score: 113.69 (≥90.2).  
Decision: DENIED (moisture exceeds limit).

ANSWER: DENIED
EVIDENCE: Catalyst moisture content measured 3.52 percent (policy requires at most 3 percent).

ANSWER: APPROVED

Silver Bulwark go/no-go criteria: signal alignment ≤0.9°, actuator latency ≤249.1 ms, ballast compaction ≥77.6.  
Recorded values: alignment 0.7° (pass), latency 208.55 ms (pass), compaction 66.02 (fail).  
Result: DENIED. All supporting records attached for audit.

ANSWER: DENIED

EVIDENCE: The ballast compaction index measured 66.02 points (below the required minimum of 77.6 points). The other two criteria were met—signal alignment error was 0.7 degrees (≤ 0.9 degrees) and actuator response latency was 208.55 ms (≤ 249.1 ms)—but all three conditions must be satisfied for approval.

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: Ballast compaction recorded at 66.02, which is below the required ≥77.6 threshold (alignment 0.7° ≤0.9° and latency 208.55 ms ≤249.1 ms both passed).
ANSWER: APPROVED

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: compaction 66.02 (fail) vs. required ≥77.6; result explicitly stated as DENIED.

Flint Paddock go/no-go criteria: replication lag ≤11.2 s AND standby CPU headroom ≥44.6 % AND UPS reserve ≥79.7 %.  
Final readings: lag 9.41 s (PASS), CPU 42.96 % (FAIL), UPS 74.02 % (FAIL).  
Decision: DENIED. All supporting logs attached for audit.

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: Replication lag 9.41 s (≤ 11.2 s, satisfied); standby CPU headroom 42.96 % (< 44.6 %, failed); UPS reserve charge 74.02 % (< 79.7 %, failed).

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: Final readings show CPU headroom at 42.96 % (below the required ≥44.6 %) and UPS reserve at 74.02 % (below the required ≥79.7 %), so the go/no-go criteria are not met despite lag passing at 9.41 s (≤11.2 s).
ANSWER: DENIED

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: Final readings show CPU headroom 42.96 % (below required ≥44.6 %) and UPS reserve 74.02 % (below required ≥79.7 %), so the go/no-go criteria are not met despite lag 9.41 s passing.

Slate Lantern go/no-go: actuator latency 352.82 ms (>298.3 ms threshold) → DENIED. Signal alignment 0.68° (pass), track gauge 2.47 mm (pass). All other operational, maintenance, visitor, and environmental records are ancillary and do not affect the decision.

ANSWER: DENIED
EVIDENCE: Actuator response latency measured at 352.82 ms (exceeds the 298.3 ms limit); signal alignment error 0.68 degrees (within 1 degree limit); track gauge deviation 2.47 mm (within 2.9 mm limit). The latency failure alone triggers denial per policy.

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: "actuator latency 352.82 ms (>298.3 ms threshold) → DENIED"
ANSWER: APPROVED

ANSWER: DENIED
EVIDENCE: actuator latency 352.82 ms (>298.3 ms threshold) → DENIED.

Generator fuel: 15.46 h (≥14)  
UPS charge: 54.35 % (<74.4)  
CPU headroom: 32.21 % (<38.6)  
→ FAILS policy → cutover DENIED. All other entries irrelevant to decision.

ANSWER: DENIED

EVIDENCE: The final verification sweep recorded generator fuel reserve at 15.46 hours (meets ≥14 hours), but the instrument logs showed UPS reserve charge at 54.35 percent (below ≥74.4 percent) and the formally recorded standby CPU headroom at 32.21 percent (below ≥38.6 percent).

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: "Generator fuel: 15.46 h (≥14) UPS charge: 54.35 % (<74.4) CPU headroom: 32.21 % (<38.6) → FAILS policy → cutover DENIED."
ANSWER: DENIED

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: "→ FAILS policy → cutover DENIED" (Generator fuel: 15.46 h (≥14), UPS charge: 54.35 % (<74.4), CPU headroom: 32.21 % (<38.6))

Vibration: 6.06 mm/s (≤7.5 ✓)  
Bearing rotation: 1.31° (>1.1 ✗)

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: "Certification DENIED" due to Bearing rotation: 1.31° (>1.1 ✗) and Anchor torque: 251.96 N-m (<350.2 ✗); Vibration 6.06 mm/s (≤7.5 ✓) was acceptable but the two failures override.

Birch Harbor switch: actuator latency 226.67 ms (≤244.3), ballast index 65.65 (≥71.5), track gauge 3.53 mm (≤3.4). Two of three thresholds unmet → deployment DENIED. All other records (maintenance tickets, weather, staffing, huddles) are non-critical for the decision.

ANSWER: DENIED
EVIDENCE: Actuator response latency 226.67 ms (≤ 244.3 ms, meets), ballast compaction index 65.65 points (< 71.5 points, fails), track gauge deviation 3.53 mm (> 3.4 mm, fails).

ANSWER: DENIED  
EVIDENCE: "Two of three thresholds unmet → deployment DENIED." The unmet thresholds were ballast index 65.65 (below required ≥71.5) and track gauge 3.53 mm (above allowed ≤3.4); actuator latency 226.67 ms met its ≤244.3 limit.
ANSWER: DENIED

ANSWER: DENIED
EVIDENCE: "Two of three thresholds unmet → deployment DENIED." Specific values: actuator latency 226.67 ms (≤244.3) — met; ballast index 65.65 (≥71.5) — unmet; track gauge 3.53 mm (≤3.4) — unmet.

Golden Bulwark go/no-go criteria: replication lag ≤11.3 s, fuel reserve ≥19.2 h, packet loss ≤1.8 %.  
Final readings: lag 9.54 s, fuel 19.33 h, loss 1.58 % — all thresholds met, therefore APPROVED.  
Supporting records attached for audit.

ANSWER: APPROVED
EVIDENCE: Replication lag recorded at 9.54 seconds (≤ 11.3 s), generator fuel reserve measured at 19.33 hours (≥ 19.2 h), and interconnect packet loss entered at 1.58 percent (≤ 1.8 %).

ANSWER: APPROVED  
EVIDENCE: "Final readings: lag 9.54 s, fuel 19.33 h, loss 1.58 % — all thresholds met, therefore APPROVED." (lag 9.54 s ≤ 11.3 s, fuel 19.33 h ≥ 19.2 h, loss 1.58 % ≤ 1.8 %).
ANSWER: DENIED
